summary: Iteration 9 backend+frontend testing. Attribution module (track-visit channel detection for instagram_ads/google_ads/instagram_organic/direct + /stats admin + admin guard), Orders persist attribution snapshot on create, Members CRUD + /stats (segments vip/returning/new/prospect, acquisition_by_channel), Hepsiburada settings save + masked password + test-connection Turkish error on missing creds, Temu/MNG test-connection Turkish errors, Categories HB/Temu ID+name persistence. 19/19 pytest cases PASSED. Frontend: login OK, new 9-group menu (Dashboard|Katalog|Siparisler|Uretim|Uyeler|Icerik|Pazarlama|Entegrasyonlar|Ayarlar) renders, /admin/uyeler shows 6 stat cards (TOPLAM UYE/SON 30 GUN/VIP/SADIK/YENI/ADAY) + member list + Yeni Uye button, /admin/kaynak renders 4 totals + Kanal Bazinda Gelir/Ziyaret charts + Kampanyalar table + UTM how-to help block, /admin/hepsiburada-eslestir shows Basic Auth form (Merchant ID + API Kullanici Adi + API Sifre) + Kaydet + Baglantiyi Test Et + Kategori Eslestirme table, /admin/temu-eslestir loads with Shop/App/Secret fields.

issues: Stat card label still says 'Toplam Musteri' (customer) but user explicitly requested 'uye' (member) terminology. All other pages correctly use Uye. Consider renaming Dashboard card to 'Toplam Uye' for consistency.

action_items: Minor: Rename Dashboard 'Toplam Musteri' card -> 'Toplam Uye' for terminology consistency (user wanted 'uye' everywhere).
critical_code_review_comments: members.py /stats performs per-user aggregation loop over orders collection (N+1 pattern). For large member base this will be slow; consider single $facet/group pipeline joining orders->user_id.

attribution.detect_channel maps utm_medium='social' (without 'paid_') to *_organic which is counter-intuitive for 'utm_medium=social' usually meaning paid social; document or align with GA4 semantics.

orders.create returns {order_id, order_number, message} but attribution-only flows like POST /api/orders are exposed without auth (user_id falls back to None); consider rate-limiting or CAPTCHA on the public endpoint to prevent order spam.
integrations.py still ~3500 lines (iteration 8 comment still valid) - should be split by provider.
attribution.stats 'by_channel' uses {$ifNull: ['$total', 0]} but does not filter out cancelled/refunded orders, inflating revenue figures.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 5 new pages + menu + login verified via Playwright)

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral test data cleaned up in-test: 1 attribution order (deleted), 2 members test_mem_*/test_dup_* (deleted). HB settings reset to empty strings after mask test. Attribution sessions remain in DB (track-visit is public - by design).
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration9 regression file: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration9_attribution_members.py. BASE read from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL or /app/frontend/.env. Login via query params. POST /api/orders is PUBLIC (no auth required) - stores attribution from session_id. Channel detection: utm_source=instagram+utm_medium=paid_social -> instagram_ads; utm_source=google+utm_medium=cpc -> google_ads; referrer=instagram.com -> instagram_organic; nothing -> direct. HB settings endpoint returns password='********' when set, '' when empty. Members endpoint lowercases emails on create. Pages verified: /admin/uyeler, /admin/kaynak, /admin/hepsiburada-eslestir, /admin/temu-eslestir.
rca_of_the_issue: None - all requested endpoints and pages working correctly. One minor UX inconsistency (Dashboard still uses 'Toplam Musteri' while project-wide terminology should be 'Uye').
